channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dumped waste blocking pavement, 8th May
item.description:
Broken bin bag full of rotten waste is blocking the pavement.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bastion Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Two mattresses dumped, 8th May
item.description:
Flytippers have dumped two mattresses at the bottom of church manor way in front of the gates of Plumstead gardens

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Church Manorway, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unresolved (ref: 9400907), 8th May
item.description:
Received another false "resolved" report this today which makes this the fifth posting since late last year. The items in question still remain as shown in the attached images. Perhaps I can speak to a council member directly and not an automated reply?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kingsland Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping of bed frame/furniture, 8th May
item.description:
Please can this be removed, it’s creating an eye sore and also could fall on passing pedestrians

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Englefield Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflow bin, 8th May
item.description:
This bin is always full with trash overflowing and scattered everywhere.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wickham Lane, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped rubbish again, 8th May
item.description:
Dumped rubbish + broken chairs & glass panel has been here for weeks now. Prime flu tipping spot!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Alconbury Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: House hold Rubish and builders Rubish, 8th May
item.description:
The Rubbish has been here for a while, from household rubbish to builders aswell, rats have been running around because of bags with rotten food, kids walk everyday on the way to school and back. There wasnt this much rubbish 3 months ago, but the longer it stays the bigger problem it will become. l would ask for your help in resolving this situation as soon as possible.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Herbert Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 8th May
item.description:
Dead fox has been placed in a black bag outside the front of 11
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Osbaldeston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Woman chucked litter into our front porch., 8th May
item.description:
A blonde woman in a medium-dark red jumper and light blue jeans came through Ashenden Road from Glyn Road at 14:25 on the 8th May and threw her litter over into our front garden, and then promptly left through Roding road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ashenden Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress behind library, 8th May
item.description:
Mattress dumped behind Camberwell Green library. Been there a month.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48, Camberwell Green, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing dog poo bin, 8th May
item.description:
Empty bin

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ladysmith Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle parked outside residential property for over one month, 8th May
item.description:
A vehicle has been parked on this public road outside my house for more than a month without being moved. It is taking up limited residential parking space and has made it very difficult for residents to find parking nearby. The vehicle appears to have been left unattended for a long period. Please could this be investigated and checked to see whether it has been abandoned.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Station Road North, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Household rubbish scattered across pavement, 8th May
item.description:
Household rubbish left out in plastic bags, now strewn over the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ledrington Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glengall Road near 55, 8th May
item.description:
Litter and a computer screen smashed on the pavement
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Glengall Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tagging, 8th May
item.description:
Tagging

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ladysmith Road, Enfield

item.category: Inoffensive up to 2 metres off the ground

item.title: General waste fly tipping at end of Bowley House bin stores, 8th May

item.title: Flytipping, 8th May
item.description:
Big fridge just found outside of Nightingale Place.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nightingale Vale, Woolwich

item.title: Overflowing litter bin on Woolwich Road alongside petrol station, 8th May
item.description:
The bin is so full the litter is gathering behind the bus stop, making this an unsightly, umpleasant and hazardous place to wait for a bus

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Path blocked, 8th May
item.description:
Tree needs cutting back

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Churchbury Lane, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No.11 flytipping again, 8th May
item.description:
Residents at no. 11 have had these black bags filled with garden rubbish on the public footpath for almost 2 weeks after being reported for having their bins on the paths for months. Today the residents of no. 11 have dumped their black bags across the opposite side of the road on the grass verge. Please make these people change their behaviour which is ruining.it for the rest of us who follow the council rules

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 11, Chipperfield Road, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
